question: Create a class Student having following private data members: string roll-number, Int age, Float cgpa. Create a public member function print in Student class which prints a Student info in following format: roll#/age/cgpa (e.g. 21L-1234/ 25/ 3.24 for std_1). Write a default Constructor of Student that initializes the objects with the default values (21L-1234, 25, 3.24) and prints a message "Default Constructor Called". Write an overloaded/parameterized Constructor of Student class that initializes the data members of student class with the received parameters (r , a and c) and prints "Overloaded/parameterized Constructor Called". Create a public function getInput in your student class that takes input from user to populate a Student object. Create separate setter functions for each data member and similarly getter functions for each data member. Dynamically create an object std_3 using default constructor. Call the setter functions for std_3 and pass the relevant data into the parameters. Call the getter functions from main function for std_3 and display the data inside main function.

test_cases.expected:
Default Constructor Called
21L-1234/25/3.24
Overloaded/parameterized Constructor Called
21L-5678/19/3.46
Enter roll number: Enter age: Enter CGPA: 21L-7890/20/3.5
